content: If we frame 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory as a recursively self-improving curious agent itself, rather than a passive storage component for agents, it will outperform existing curiosity-driven reinforcement learning frameworks at retaining and prioritizing high-value exploratory experiences for long-horizon tasks. This re-frames memory from a supporting utility into an active decision-maker that drives its own information-seeking to refine the agent's world model.
